title: GatesSpec
description:
A gate specification loaded from `gates.toml` in a directory-based spec.

Parallel to [`crate::Spec`] but designed for the directory layout where
criteria live in `gates.toml` alongside a `spec.toml`.

criteria.description: Gate criteria that must be satisfied.

depends.description:
Spec slugs this gate spec depends on. Used for dependency ordering and cycle detection.
Each entry is a slug — the spec file name without extension (e.g. `"auth-flow"`
for `specs/auth-flow.toml`). Entries must be unique and non-empty.

description.description: Human-readable description. Defaults to empty string.

milestone.description:
Optional milestone slug this spec belongs to (e.g. `"my-feature"`).

When set, this spec is associated with a milestone tracked in
`.assay/milestones/<slug>.toml`. Omitted from serialized output when absent.

name.description: Display name for this gate spec (must match directory name / feature spec name).

order.description:
Optional ordering hint within the milestone's chunk sequence.

Lower values sort earlier. Omitted from serialized output when absent.

Criterion.description:
A single acceptance criterion attached to a spec.

Each criterion has a name, description, and an optional shell command
that can verify it programmatically. When `kind` is `AgentReport`, the
criterion is evaluated by an agent using the `prompt` field for guidance.

cmd.description:
Optional shell command to verify this criterion.
Omitted from serialized output when `None`.
Mutually exclusive with `kind = AgentReport`.

description.description: Detailed description of what this criterion checks.

enforcement.description:
Enforcement level override for this criterion. `None` means "use the
spec-level default from `[gate]` section" (which itself defaults to `required`).

kind.description:
Criterion evaluation kind. When set to `AgentReport`, this criterion
is evaluated by an agent (not a shell command). Mutually exclusive
with `cmd` and `path`.

path.description:
Optional file path to check for existence.
When set (and `cmd` is `None`), the criterion evaluates as a `FileExists` gate.

prompt.description:
Instruction prompt for agent-evaluated criteria.
Provides guidance to the agent on what to evaluate.

requirements.description: Requirement IDs this criterion traces to (e.g., `["REQ-FUNC-001"]`).

timeout.description:
Optional timeout in seconds for this criterion's command.
Overrides the global default but is overridden by CLI `--timeout`.

when.description:
Declares when this criterion is evaluated. `None` is equivalent to
`Some(When::SessionEnd)` — evaluation happens at session end. When
set to `Some(When::AfterToolCalls { .. })` or `Some(When::OnEvent { .. })`,
the criterion becomes a mid-session checkpoint.

CriterionKind.description:
The kind of evaluation a criterion uses.

When set on a criterion, determines how it is evaluated.
`AgentReport` means the criterion is evaluated by an agent
via structured reasoning rather than a shell command.

oneOf.description: Evaluated by an agent via structured reasoning.

Criterion evaluated by counting events of a given type in the agent
session log. Parameters mirror `GateKind::EventCount`.

event_type.description: The event type to count, matching an `AgentEvent` serde tag.

max.description: Inclusive upper bound on the matched event count. `None` means no upper bound.

min.description: Inclusive lower bound on the matched event count. `None` means no lower bound.

oneOf.description: Criterion that passes only when no tool returned an error during the session.

Enforcement.description:
Enforcement level for a gate criterion.

Determines whether a criterion failure blocks the gate (required)
or is informational only (advisory).

oneOf.description: Failure blocks the gate. This is the default.
Failure is informational; does not block the gate.

GateSection.description:
Gate-level configuration section.

Parsed from `[gate]` in spec TOML files. Provides spec-wide defaults
that individual criteria can override.

enforcement.description: Default enforcement level for all criteria in this spec.

When.description:
Declares when a gate criterion is evaluated.

Spec authors use this to designate cheap event-based criteria as
mid-session checkpoints while keeping expensive command/file gates
at session end.

# Warning on command/file gates at checkpoints

Using `AfterToolCalls` or `OnEvent` with a `cmd`- or `path`-based
criterion evaluates against a **partial working directory**. Prefer
`EventCount` and `NoToolErrors` criteria for checkpoints.

oneOf.description: Evaluate at the end of the session (default behavior).

Evaluate each time the agent has emitted N tool calls since the
last evaluation. The pipeline fires this trigger at the Nth tool
call (exact match, not "at least N").

n.description: Tool-call threshold at which to evaluate.

oneOf.description:
Evaluate when the most-recent event in the stream has a serde
type tag matching `event_type` (e.g. `"tool_called"`).

event_type.description: Event type tag to match (matches `AgentEvent` serde tag).
